reforms.description: Draft legislation was finalized in May 1988 to increase competition in the telecommunications sector, allowing private companies to offer equipment and network services. The Federal Government also approved the draft on limited deregulation of the telecommunications system, enabling initial competition in fringe services.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing competition in the telecommunications sector is expected to enhance innovation and efficiency, benefiting the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: Draft legislation was finalized and approved.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant structural change in the telecommunications sector.
reforms.source_quote: Under the draft legislation, finalised in May 1988, which followed closely the relatively modest recommendations of a commission of experts (Witte Report), room for competition would initially be increased in data services.

reforms.description: The Employment Promotion Law was passed in 1985 to increase the possibilities for concluding employment contracts of limited duration and improve employment opportunities for part-time job seekers.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing employment opportunities is likely to improve human capital and productivity in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The law was passed and is in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform is significant but not a major overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: A limited, though important, step to increasing labour market flexibility has been made in 1985, however, when the Employment Promotion Law was passed.

reforms.description: The Federal Government announced a special 4- to 5-year programme worth DM 1 billion aimed at improving the economic structure of regions with declining iron and steel industries.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Targeting economic improvement in declining industries is expected to revitalize these regions and enhance overall economic performance.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme was announced by the Federal Government.
reforms.importance_rationale: The programme is significant for targeted regional development.
reforms.source_quote: The Federal Government announces a special 4- to 5-year programme (worth DM 1 billion) aimed at improving the economic structure of regions with declining iron and steel industries.
